RE: Learning JavaScript From Zero — EP.0: The Table of Contents (and Why I Started)
RE：從零開始的學習 JS 生活
第 11 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

This is the second series I wrote for the iThome Ironman challenge, and it's all about JavaScript — but aimed squarely at beginners. I mean, it's literally called "from zero." This post is the intro and the full table of contents for the whole series.
ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 14 - Model Basics, Part 2: Inserting Data

Last time we got all the Model boilerplate ready. Now it's time to actually write some basic data operations. In this post we wire up a Model inside the controller, instantiate it, and insert a real row into the think_user table — then check whether the save actually worked using the boolean that save() returns.
ThinkPHP Day 13 — Wiring Up the Model: Routes, Controllers, and Your First Form

From here on we start tying together the pieces we covered earlier to do some basic Model operations. This time we'll bring the Controller and Route into the mix — register a couple of routes, build a small HTML form, and watch ThinkPHP grab the form fields and hand them straight to our Controller method.
Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 12 - Routing

We've covered the basic MVC structure and the core definitions, so now it's time to get our hands dirty. But before we do, there's one more thing we need to understand first - Route. For modern development, routing is a really important foundation, and ThinkPHP makes it pretty straightforward once you get the hang of it.
Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 11 - Defining Models

This chapter is a little shorter. It mostly covers the basics of defining a Model. In normal development you don't really tweak the Model much, so you'll notice there genuinely isn't a whole lot here. We'll walk through defining a Model, connecting to the database, and pointing a Model at a specific table.
Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 10 - The View

This time we're looking at the View part of MVC. Last time we covered the basics of the Controller, so now it's the View's turn, and we'll get to the Model afterwards. Along the way we'll also pick up a little bit of HTML templating with ThinkPHP's built-in template engine.
Deploying articles by hand has been getting old, so I'm switching Hexo deploy to fully automated.
Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch Day 9 - Writing Your First Controller

Now that we understand the MVC architecture, it's time to start writing some code. The first thing we'll write is the Controller. But before we get there, there are a couple of settings we need to tweak so development goes a lot smoother. In this post we'll turn on debug mode, define a basic Controller, and walk through how ThinkPHP maps URLs to your controllers.
ThinkPHP Day 8: What MVC Actually Means (and Why You Should Care)

Before we get into the later chapters, I have to pump the brakes for a second and say sorry — we need to understand the MVC (Model-View-Controller) pattern first. This chapter walks through what MVC actually is, because the concept makes everything we build later way easier to follow. We'll go through what each letter means, how the flow actually works, and why splitting your code this way saves you from future pain.
ThinkPHP Day 7: Where Does Everything Live? A Tour of the Directory Structure

We've already got ThinkPHP 5.1 installed, but instead of jumping straight into building stuff, let's slow down for a second. If you don't understand the directory structure, you'll have no idea where things are — or worse, where they're supposed to go. So in this chapter we'll walk through ThinkPHP's folder layout, look at the entry files, and even cover how to rename the core folder (and why I'd really rather you didn't).
